title: System Stacking

content.statement: One action should serve multiple goals.
content.question: What one action could you design to serve 3 goals instead of 1?
sop: List your top 5 recurring actions and the single goal each currently serves.
For each, ask what other goal (health, brand, relationships, revenue) it could also serve with a small tweak.
Rate the Outputs per Action for your top candidate before and after the redesign (see calculation, page 4)
Redesign the action to intentionally hit the extra goal(s) at the same time.
Re-audit monthly, as goals shift, some stacked actions will need to be re-paired.
questions: What one action could you design to serve 3 goals instead of 1?
Which single-purpose task in your week could be redesigned to also serve a second goal for free?
What are you currently doing separately that could be combined into one action without losing either result?
content.equation_example: One action serves 3 goals and it took 1 action. Leverage = 3 ÷ 1 = 3.0. Higher means each action does more work at the same time cost; 1.0 means you're spending a separate action on each thing when they could have been combined.
content.closing_statement: One action, one goal is the expensive default.

caption:
Principle Nº 157: System Stacking

One action should serve multiple goals.

Ask yourself: What one action could you design to serve 3 goals instead of 1?
